title: Sous beans (pickled beans)
excerpt: I made sous beans yesterday. I was asked for the recipe. I'm happy to share it Ingredients 1kg butter beans – or red speckled beans 750 ml vinegar 500 ml sugar 5 ml salt 10 ml maizena Method Put the beans in a bowl overnight and cover with cold water Rinse the …
html:
I made sous beans yesterday. I was asked for the recipe. I'm happy to share it

Ingredients

  • 1kg butter beans – or red speckled beans
  • 750 ml vinegar
  • 500 ml sugar
  • 5 ml salt
  • 10 ml maizena

Method

  1. Put the beans in a bowl overnight and cover with cold water
  2. Rinse the beans well and put them in a pot. Add enough water so the beans are covered
    NO SALT
  3. Cook until your beans are soft. I set the lid at an angle. The water starts to boil off. This can take a while.
    The beans mustn't cook to mush
  4. Add the vinegar, salt and sugar. Stir through and let it simmer for 20 minutes. Now you need to taste it. If it's too sweet, add more vinegar, too sour, more sugar. It should have a nice tangy taste
  5. Mix the maizena with 100ml water and stir into your beans to thicken the sauce
  6. Pour your beans into sterilised bottles.
    Enjoy
    Well worth the effort – I promise.
